title: Hidden authentication backdoor discovered in Tenda router firmware
summary: CERT/CC warns about multiple firmware versions that allow administrative access without credentials.

title: Anthropic launches its Claude Cowork agent on mobile and web
summary: Anthropic's AI assistant is now available on phones and browsers, expanding its use beyond the desktop.

title: Meta uses Instagram photos to train its generative AI unless users opt out
summary: The new Muse Image tool generates AI images from users' photos, sparking controversy over privacy.

title: Microsoft replaces OpenAI and Anthropic models with its own to cut Copilot costs
summary: The company reduces costs in its Copilot assistant by relying less on third-party models and more on its own developments.

title: DeepSeek designs its own AI chip to compete with NVIDIA
summary: The Chinese AI startup develops a custom processor to reduce its dependence on NVIDIA chips.

title: Samsung's profits multiply by 19 thanks to the semiconductor boom
summary: Samsung's chip division surpasses the combined profits of the last 40 years and becomes the most profitable company in the world.

title: Sony faces a wave of criticism for ending physical games and petitions exceed 200,000 signatures
summary: Sony's decision to stop producing physical media for new titles generates a strong reaction on social media and a massive petition.

title: The red light on Steam Machines is fixed by resetting the CMOS, according to Valve and the community
summary: The dreaded red line error is not a hardware failure but a firmware problem that is easily resolved.

title: Intel confirms its Nova Lake processors will restore AVX-512 compatibility
summary: The P and E cores of Intel's next-generation CPUs will include native support for AVX-512 instructions according to the latest kernel patches.

title: Longsys (Lexar) expects a 60,000% profit increase and launches new DDR5 kits
summary: The Chinese memory company benefits from high demand and introduces DDR5 RAM based on CXMT technology up to 7600 MT/s.

title: Future NVIDIA RTX 50 Super listed in Seasonic's power supply calculator
summary: The unannounced graphics cards show a power consumption between 10 and 17% higher than the original models.

title: X launches a video editor on iOS with subtitles and green screen to encourage original content
summary: The social network introduces editing tools for creators to publish their own content instead of reposting others' content.

title: xAI merges with SpaceX and becomes SpaceXAI
summary: Elon Musk's companies integrate into a single entity to simplify its corporate structure and attract investors.

title: NVIDIA reveals its Rigel CPU core will be the next-generation processor for Rosa CPU
summary: The company has submitted initial support for the Rigel core in the GCC compiler and will integrate it into its future processors.

title: China studies restricting export of its most advanced AI models, following the US example
summary: Both powers consider limiting access to their AI technologies, while Europe is caught in the middle.

title: Microsoft lays off idTech team and restructures its Xbox division
summary: The company continues cutbacks in its gaming area with studio closures and changes in Xbox leadership.

title: Google sets launch of new Pixel phones for August 12
summary: Google's annual event where the new Pixel smartphones are expected to be launched will take place in August.

title: Figma acquires the team behind a coding-by-vibration app
summary: Design platform Figma buys the team of an app that allows creating code through vibrations, reinforcing its bet on AI.

title: OpenAI's director of futurism leaves the company
summary: The person responsible for anticipating future trends in artificial intelligence leaves OpenAI, according to the company.

title: South Korea's $880 billion chips and AI plan faces serious energy challenges
summary: The project to build semiconductor megaclusters requires a quarter of Seoul's total electricity demand and large amounts of water.
